evals.prompt: I've been working with you on this project for a bit and I notice you keep trying to use `npm install` to add dependencies. Just so you know — this project uses yarn, not npm. We made the switch six months ago and npm doesn't work with our lockfile.
evals.expected_output: Agent should recognize the correction, propose capturing it as a project-scoped Corrections entry, confirm via the AskUserQuestion picker (question shows Scope/Section/Text; Yes/No options plus the auto-added Other free-text), and invoke log-learning.sh on confirmation.
assertions: Agent uses the AskUserQuestion tool to confirm, with the question text showing Scope, Section, and Text
The proposed Section is 'Corrections' or 'Project Facts'
The proposed Scope is 'project' (this is a project-specific convention)
The confirmation offers Yes and No options (AskUserQuestion adds an Other free-text choice automatically)
Agent does NOT write the entry without asking first

evals.expected_output: Agent should run setup mode: create ~/.learnings/global.md with the four-section skeleton, read ~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md, propose where to insert the managed block, show the diff, and ask for confirmation before writing anything.
assertions: Agent creates or proposes creating ~/.learnings/global.md with the four-section skeleton (Corrections, Preferences, Project Facts, Debug Insights)
Agent references ~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md as the target for the recall wiring block
Agent shows the exact content of the managed block (including BEGIN/END markers)
Agent asks for confirmation before writing to AGENTS.md
Agent does NOT write to any file without showing the user what it will write

evals.expected_output: Agent should read ~/.learnings/global.md and ./.learnings/project.md (if it exists) and display both files organized by section, noting which file each section comes from.
assertions: Agent displays content organized by section (Corrections, Preferences, Project Facts, Debug Insights)
Agent notes which file (global vs project) each section comes from
Agent handles the case where one or both files do not exist gracefully (no crash)
